title: Guided Tour of Kurukshetra Dhama by Govind Krsna Das with aerial drone footage.
speaker: Yatri

segments.text: Be sure to subscribe to our channel. We publish new videos every day.
Thank you.
My dear Akul, lots of people ask us when is the best time to come to Kurukshetra?
If you could elaborate about this.
Well, Kurukshetra is open hearted.
It starts throughout the year.
But during the month of Gita Jayanti, at the month of December, it is the best time to
visit Kurukshetra Dham.
Tell us something about Gita Jayanti.
You mentioned Gita Jayanti.
So what does Gita Jayanti signify?
Gita Jayanti signifies when the Lord chose to give us the syllabus of life.
The Lord chose to sing his song Bhagwat Gita.
Gita means to sing.
The Lord has sung his song, which means he has given his instructions very melodiously
to Arjuna.
Okay, so Gita Jayanti signifies the appearance, the birth of Bhagwat Gita.
Yes.
So Gita Jayanti, anything specific that the government of Haryana does to, you know, make
this festival even more big?
Well, you'll be very happy to know that the Haryana government is working day and night
for this festival.
And it has taken this festival from a state festival to an international festival.
Now the name of this festival is called International Gita Mahotsav.
It has crossed the borders above Haryana and gone all across the world.
But Prabhupada did this way back when he was here.
This is interesting.
But it is nice to see the government is putting their heart in to make this a very global
festival.
So how do they do this?
What all activities do they do?
Are there a lot of exhibitions, dramas, or how have they made this festival come to an
international level?
Could you just tell us what all happens at this time?
Well, as you know, anything big takes lots of effort.
It takes a lot of effort.
It takes a lot of effort.
Lots of planning, preparation, you know, systematic of assessment.
So likewise, the KDB, the Kurukshetra Development Board, and the Haryana government, they have
spent sleepless nights for over three months to get this power-packed Gita Jayanti festival
ready for the whole country.
And so much has happened, late night meetings with authorities, and one very good thing
that they're doing.
It's a lot.
They are involving all the spiritual institutions, and by the way, in which ISKCON is spearheading
all of them.
Wow.
So, you're going to be giving the email to now, introduce the festival to us, and then
we will give you all the details.
You know, there are a number of other things that we're going to be doing.
We're going to be having the Kurukshetra girls, the Kurukshetra dance, the Kurukshetra
awards, the Kurukshetra marathons, the Kurukshetra Dhanas, the Kurukshetra festival, and the
Kurukshetra soapboxes.
Wow!
Awesome.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Awesome!
Go ahead.
Go ahead.
All right.
can be soaking inside the Gita Mahatmya or the prayers and the vani of the Bhagavad Gita.
Okay. Number two, artists and devotees from all across the globe are coming and performing and
giving their cultured dance performances. Wow. Number three, as you see during the Brahma
Sova is filled with over 25 lakh pilgrims. Wow. Likewise, every stage and lots of various
culture, you know, flavor is there in Kursha Duryodhana during the Gita Jayanti Mahotsava.
Likewise, if you see towards my left, this is the... There? Yes, this is the Maha Aarti venue.
Okay. Every evening like in Haridwar, you have been to Haridwar, there are big, big Aartis.
Correct. Likewise, this place is where the VIP Aarti happens of Lord Brahma and Lord Krishna
because this is the very spot where Lord Brahma started to do his penance. So you mean to say
like I've seen in Haridwar, you know, there are like big D lamps and they take it like this and
then, you know, they show Aarti.
This happens over here? Yes, but there's a twist. In this Aarti, there are also mantras from the Bhagavad
Gita. Wow. That is the add-on flavor. This is catchy. On the Bhagavad Gita day, every year
in Kurukshetra, there are 18,000 boys chanting the Bhagavad Gita at the same time, which is the world
record of the most number of students reciting the Bhagavad Gita in one go. This is really, really big.
Oh, cooker.
Ya Dhail Dahi
Dhail Dahi
Dhail Dahi
So before we, you know, wind this up, so you told us 18,000 students sit at one place and on the day of blockchain, Athapeni goes like this.
Now does that agree? Yes.
18,000 students sit at one place and on the day of Bhagavad Gita they chant the shlokas, correct?
Yes.
And there is Maha Aartis that happen and the government of Haryana is playing a pivotal role, you know,
to bring this festival on an international level.
Just, you know, a few more to, like, you know, end this up.
What all happens also?
Well, out of many activities, first there is the International Gita Conference.
Correct.
In which the best speakers on the Bhagavad Gita are welcomed from across the world and to be asked to speak on.
Then there are painting competitions, kulaar making competition, fancy business competition.
And it is like the meeting time.
The whole of Haryana or the state, the local state Haryana, which is named after Lord Hari or Krishna.
Correct.
Gathers and it's like a very festive time.
Like New Year's is coming up, Christmas is coming, but our New Year's and Christmas is Gita Jayanti.
A festival of love, meeting, food, spirituality, Bhagavad Gita with songs and dances.
And kulaar.
Culture mixing.
Though we have so many differences culture-wise, but coming together under the roof of Bhagavad Gita and uniting as one.
Ah, ah ah ah ah.
Bhambmo nā bhavah māt bhaktok vā refundā mām na māsū
jejam manmāsum amevayaśa-siutai vām
ātmannā māt varāyaetti
Ah.
mātman-māt parāyana
This is, by the way, Asia's largest water tank, for those of you who don't know.
When you Google Brahma Sarovara, you will see a big tract of water on the map, which
is Brahma Sarovara.
So, every section in this, as you see, every section has one presentation from a specific
country or a state or some part of the society showcasing their heritage, natural heritage.
Their culture, in food, in clothing.
Yes, and mind you, ISKCON is also here, in which we are showcasing Prabhupada's
Gita as it is, Harinam, and we are distributing free Prasad to over 3,000 pilgrims every day,
free of cost.
So, whether food, culture, dance, painting, we got it all for you.
Just waiting for you.
Ladies and gentlemen, now you know when is the best time to come to Kurukshetra.
It is in December, Gita Jayanti is the day when Krishna spoke the Bhagavad Gita.
Atulji just told us about all the activities that are happening in Kurukshetra and how
the Haryana government has taken this festival to an international level.
So next time, whenever you want to know when is the best time to go to Kurukshetra, please
come to Kurukshetra.
You need to tell your friends, your relatives, you tell them it is in the month of December
at the time of Gita Jayanti when there is so much happening in Kurukshetra.
We welcome you to come down to this land of the Mahabharata, to the birthplace of the
Bhagavad Gita and soak your consciousness into this divine, transcendental knowledge
of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Sri Krishna.
That's it.
Waiting for you all.
See you soon.
Hare Krishna.
Thank you very much.
